# System Design {#sec-system-design}

This chapter presents the comprehensive system design of UpHeal, covering requirements analysis, architectural decisions, detailed diagrams, and security considerations.

## Functional Requirements {#sec-functional-requirements}

The following functional requirements define the core capabilities of the UpHeal system:

-   **FR-01**: The system shall allow users to register and authenticate securely.
-   **FR-02**: The system shall provide an AI-powered conversational interface that responds to the user's mental health concerns.
-   **FR-03**: The system shall detect the user's emotional state from text and voice input.
-   **FR-04**: The system shall adapt its response style based on the detected emotion.
-   **FR-05**: The system shall allow users to log their mood on a daily basis.
-   **FR-06**: The system shall provide gamification elements including streaks, badges, and progress tracking.
-   **FR-07**: The system shall integrate a retrieval-augmented generation pipeline that grounds LLM responses in curated clinical knowledge.
-   **FR-08**: The system shall support speech-to-text input via the Whisper model.
-   **FR-09**: The system shall store conversation history securely for user review.
-   **FR-10**: The system shall provide an emergency resources page with crisis helpline information.

## Non-Functional Requirements {#sec-non-functional-requirements}

-   **NFR-01**: Response latency for chat interactions shall not exceed 5 seconds under normal conditions.
-   **NFR-02**: The system shall comply with data protection regulations (GDPR/Egyptian DPL).
-   **NFR-03**: The application shall be cross-platform (Android and iOS).
-   **NFR-04**: The system shall maintain 99.5% uptime for backend services.
-   **NFR-05**: All sensitive data shall be encrypted at rest and in transit.

## Use Case Diagram {#sec-use-case}

```{mermaid}
graph TD
    A[User] --> B[Register/Login]
    A --> C[Start Chat Session]
    A --> D[Log Mood]
    A --> E[View Progress & Badges]
    A --> F[Use Voice Input]
    A --> G[Access Emergency Resources]
    
    H[System] --> I[Classify Emotion]
    H --> J[Generate RAG-Augmented Response]
    H --> K[Update Gamification State]
    H --> L[Store Conversation]
```

## Sequence Diagram — Chat Interaction {#sec-sequence-diagram}

```{mermaid}
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User
    participant F as Flutter App
    participant B as FastAPI Backend
    participant S as Supabase
    participant R as RunPod GPU
    participant V as Vector DB

    U->>F: Send message
    F->>B: POST /chat {user_id, message}
    B->>R: Emotion classification request
    R-->>B: Detected emotion label
    B->>V: Retrieve relevant context (RAG)
    V-->>B: Top-k passages
    B->>R: LLM generation with context
    R-->>B: Generated response
    B->>S: Store conversation turn
    B-->>F: Response + emotion + badge update
    F-->>U: Display response
```

## Functional Flow Diagram {#sec-functional-flow}

```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
    A[User Opens App] --> B{Authenticated?}
    B -->|No| C[Login / Register]
    B -->|Yes| D[Home Dashboard]
    C --> D
    D --> E[Select Action]
    E --> F[Chat Session]
    E --> G[Mood Logger]
    E --> H[Progress View]
    E --> I[Emergency Resources]
    F --> J[Send Message]
    J --> K[Emotion Classification]
    K --> L[RAG Context Retrieval]
    L --> M[LLM Response Generation]
    M --> N[Display Response]
    N --> O[Update Gamification]
    O --> F
    G --> P[Save Mood Entry]
    P --> O
```

## System Architecture {#sec-architecture}

The UpHeal system follows a layered microservices architecture:

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
    subgraph Frontend
        A[Flutter Mobile App]
    end
    subgraph Backend
        B[FastAPI Server]
        C[Supabase Auth + DB]
    end
    subgraph AI Services
        D[RunPod GPU]
        D1[Whisper STT]
        D2[Gemma LLM]
        D3[Emotion Classifier]
    end
    subgraph Knowledge
        E[Vector Database]
        F[Clinical Knowledge Base]
    end

    A --> B
    B --> C
    B --> D
    D --> D1
    D --> D2
    D --> D3
    D2 --> E
    E --> F
```

## AI/ML Pipeline Design {#sec-ai-pipeline}

The AI pipeline processes user input through three stages:

1.  **Input Processing**: Text is received directly; voice input is transcribed using Whisper.
2.  **Emotion Classification**: The transcribed or typed text is passed through a fine-tuned emotion classifier to detect labels such as *anxious*, *sad*, *angry*, *neutral*, and *hopeful*.
3.  **Response Generation**: The classified emotion and user message are used to retrieve relevant clinical passages from the vector database. These passages, along with the conversation context, are fed into Gemma LLM via a structured prompt to generate a grounded, empathetic response.

## Hardware Considerations {#sec-hardware}

| Component | Specification |
|-----------|------|
| GPU (AI Inference) | NVIDIA A100 80GB — RunPod Cloud |
| Backend Server | DigitalOcean Droplet — 4 vCPU, 8GB RAM |
| Database | Supabase managed PostgreSQL |
| Client Device | Android 10+ / iOS 15+ with 4GB RAM minimum |

Table: Hardware and infrastructure specifications. {#tbl-hardware}

## Security Considerations {#sec-security}

-   **Authentication**: Supabase Auth with JWT tokens and OAuth2 support.
-   **Data encryption**: TLS 1.3 in transit; AES-256 at rest.
-   **Access control**: Role-based access control (RBAC) with Row-Level Security (RLS) in Supabase.
-   **Data minimization**: Only necessary user data is collected; conversation logs are anonymized for analytics.
-   **Emergency protocol**: Crisis keywords trigger a mandatory redirect to emergency resources page with helpline numbers.
-   **Compliance**: Designed with GDPR and Egyptian Data Protection Law principles in mind.
